## Beatport Group Cookie Consent

By clicking “Accept All Cookies”, you agree to the storing of cookies on your device to enhance site navigation, analyze site usage, and assist in our marketing efforts. [Beatport Group Cookie Policy](https://support.beatport.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412316093588)

Manage Preferences Accept All Cookies

![Image 29: Beatport Group Logo](https://cdn-ukwest.onetrust.com/logos/6371edb6-d848-4f6e-a625-5f6787009ced/129701c2-f02a-4b49-8060-40a38e731544/64c699cf-1c2c-4664-a481-efcb69130bf0/Beatport_GroupLogo_Icon_300x300.png)

## Do Not Sell My Personal Information

When you visit our website, we store cookies on your browser to collect information. The information collected might relate to you, your preferences or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to and to provide a more personalized web experience. However, you can choose not to allow certain types of cookies, which may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings according to your preference. You cannot opt-out of our First Party Strictly Necessary Cookies as they are deployed in order to ensure the proper functioning of our website (such as prompting the cookie banner and remembering your settings, to log into your account, to redirect you when you log out, etc.). For more information about the First and Third Party Cookies used please follow this link. 

[Beatport Group Privacy and Cookie Policy](https://support.beatport.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412316093588)

Allow All
### Manage Consent Preferences

#### Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

#### Sale of Personal Data

- [x] Sale of Personal Data 

Under the California Consumer Privacy Act, you have the right to opt-out of the sale of your personal information to third parties. These cookies collect information for analytics and to personalize your experience with targeted ads. You may exercise your right to opt out of the sale of personal information by using this toggle switch. If you opt out we will not be able to offer you personalised ads and will not hand over your personal information to any third parties. Additionally, you may contact our legal department for further clarification about your rights as a California consumer by using this Exercise My Rights link.

If you have enabled privacy controls on your browser (such as a plugin), we have to take that as a valid request to opt-out. Therefore we would not be able to track your activity through the web. This may affect our ability to personalize ads according to your preferences.

*   ##### Social Media Cookies

- [x] Switch Label label

## Word History

Etymology

Middle English, from Old English _fīend_; akin to Old High German _fīant_ enemy, Sanskrit _pīyati_ he reviles, blames

First Known Use

before the 12th century, in the meaning defined at [sense 1a](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/fiend#h1)

Time Traveler

The first known use of _fiend_ was before the 12th century

His hands were trembling, actually trembling, as if he were some sort of coffee fiend or something.—T. Coraghessan Boyle, _The Road to Wellville_, 1993 Wodehouse may not have liked Dickens, but he certainly read him. He read like a fiend.—Christopher Hitchens, _Times Literary Supplement_, 7-13 Sept. 1990 The shameless effrontery of the fiend, at the café, pretending to forget all he had done to her, begging to take up with her again, as if nothing had happened between them a dozen years ago.—Irving Wallace, _The Plot_, 1967 a _fiend_ in human form  He's a real golf _fiend_.

Quig, _Chicago Tribune_, 17 June 2026 [](https://www.chicagotribune.com/2026/06/17/obama-presidential-center-political-buttons/)[See All Example Sentences for _fiend_](https://www.merriam-webster.com/sentences/fiend)

## Word History

Etymology

Middle English, from Old English _fīend_; akin to Old High German _fīant_ enemy, Sanskrit _pīyati_ he reviles, blames

First Known Use

before the 12th century, in the meaning defined at [sense 1a](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/fiend#h1)

Time Traveler

The first known use of _fiend_ was before the 12th century

A comprehensive clearance search means checking a variety of resources to determine whether your trademark conflicts with other existing trademarks.

You should search for trademarks that are confusingly similar to yours when used with goods and services that are the same as or related to yours. Doing this can help you avoid costly and time-consuming issues, such as:

*   A refusal to register based on a likelihood of confusion with a registered trademark for goods or services related to yours
*   An opposition proceeding to prevent registration of your United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO)-approved trademark or a cancellation proceeding against your trademark registration
*   A trademark infringement lawsuit

We strongly recommend you search for federally registered and pending trademarks that may conflict with yours before filing your federal trademark application. You can [use our free resources to do so](/trademarks/search/federal-trademark-searching "Federal trademark searching"), which can help you avoid a likelihood of confusion refusal during the application process.

We also recommend you search for common-law use of the trademark by others. Common-law rights are based solely on someone using their trademark in commerce within the United States with specific goods or services and may affect the rights provided by your federal registration if the common-law use was earlier than the use supporting your registration. Therefore, we encourage you to search the internet for third-party references to your trademark (and similar trademarks) used with goods or services that are related to your goods or services.

## A comprehensive clearance search includes:

*   [The USPTO trademark search system](https://tmsearch.uspto.gov) of federally registered and pending trademarks
*   [Trademark Official Gazette (TMOG)](https://eog-tmng.uspto.gov/): A USPTO weekly publication that includes Principal Register trademarks that received preliminary approval for registration
*   [U.S. state trademark and business registries](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/basics/state-trademark-information-links): The USPTO page for links to trademark registration information for the 50 states, as well as Washington, D.C., and Puerto Rico
*   [Domain name registries](https://www.icann.org/registrants): The Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers page for domain name registrants
*   [Madrid Monitor](https://www3.wipo.int/madrid/monitor/en/): The database of international trademark registrations processed in the Madrid System
*   [Global Brand Database](https://www.wipo.int/reference/en/branddb/): The World Intellectual Property Organization’s search engine for trademarks from national and international sources
*   [EUIPO](https://www.euipo.europa.eu/en/trade-marks/before-applying/availability): The landing page for the European Union Intellectual Property Office’s (EUIPO)
*   [TMview](https://www.tmdn.org/tmview/), a database of trademark applications and registered trademarks from all EU national IP offices, the EUIPO, and several international partner offices outside the EU
*   Internet: Check multiple search engines to look for goods and services that may conflict with yours

A comprehensive clearance search can feel overwhelming. At a minimum, be sure to check our database and the TMOG for federal applications and registrations, and also search the internet for common-law use.

For a more in-depth search, you may choose to use a trademark screening service or work with an [experienced U.S.-licensed attorney](/trademarks/basics/why-hire-private-trademark-attorney "Hiring a U.S.-licensed attorney"). An attorney can provide a clearance search and interpret the search results as well as advise you on how to apply for federal trademark registration.

**DISCLAIMER**: References to particular trademarks, service marks, certification marks, products, services, companies, or organizations appearing on this page are for illustrative and educational purposes only and do not constitute or imply endorsement by the U.S. government, the U.S. Department of Commerce, the USPTO or any other federal agency.

text:
## Find your creative flow.

Roland Zenbeats is a music creation app that keeps you in an effortless artistic flow. Get started on any mobile device or computer platform and make music anywhere, anytime. With its collection of both modern and legendary sounds, Zenbeats brings Roland’s history of innovation into a new, mobile-friendly format.

![](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_intro.png)

View the Gallery

![The app is your instrument.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature1_01.jpg)

## The app is your instrument.

Whether you’re an emerging musician or an established producer, Zenbeats makes music creation easy. Build beats, compose full multitrack songs, or sample the world around you. Wherever you are, no matter what platform or device you use, capture your creative spark with Zenbeats.

![Your home studio. Everywhere you go.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature1_02.jpg)

## Your home studio. Everywhere you go.

When you open Zenbeats, everything you need to create is immediately at your fingertips. Drop a beat with the ZR1 Drum Sampler, sketch synth ideas with the ZC1 Synthesizer, record audio tracks, and mix masterpieces on the go. And when you need some extra fuel, you can access lessons, download song templates, browse Store sounds, and more within the app.

![A universe of sound.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature1_03.jpg)

## A universe of sound.

Whatever style you’re working in, you’ll find the perfect sounds for your tracks in Zenbeats. From vintage Roland JUNO and JUPITER tones to diverse and progressive instruments for any genre, the 14,000-plus presets will keep your compositions fresh and exciting.

## ZR1. The future of beatmaking.

Our ZR1 Drum Sampler is a new kind of beat machine. Fast track your percussive prowess with seminal Roland drum tones from the TR-808, TR-909, and more. Not finding what you’re looking for? Then use your own sounds to build the kit of your dreams.

![ZR1. The future of beatmaking.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature2_01.png)

![Sample your world.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature2_02.png)

### Sample your world.

Sample and import directly into ZR1’s drum pads with the touch of a finger. Craft exotic kits with whatever is around you using an intuitive interface that makes creating fast and fun.

![Edit. Refine. Repeat.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature2_03.png)

### Edit. Refine. Repeat.

Tweak your sounds with advanced editing functionality. Manipulate and chop your one-shot samples with the slice editor and quickly crop and fade as needed.

![Beatmaking bliss.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature2_04.png)

### Beatmaking bliss.

Create complex rhythms with the deep and dynamic step sequencer, complete with accents, per-step automation, and more. Then process individual tracks with onboard effects to shape the ultimate drum sound.

![ZC1. Roland’s most advanced synth engine.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature1_04.jpg)

## ZC1. Roland’s most advanced synth engine.

ZC1 is a versatile synthesizer based on our powerful ZEN-Core Synthesis System. The free version features signature Roland synth sounds and 60 presets, driven by a sleek touch-based interface with an X/Y pad for easy sound manipulation. When you unlock the full version of ZC1, you get over 900 additional presets and 90 MFX that are all fully compatible with ZENOLOGY and supported ZEN-Core hardware.

Note: ZC1 is only supported on devices running 64-bit processors and operating systems.

![Deep MIDI and audio power at your fingertips.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature1_05.jpg)

## Deep MIDI and audio power at your fingertips.

The Zenbeats ecosystem offers tons of time-saving tools to document, edit, and refine your ideas. Quickly capture and play sounds with LoopBuilder, or use a traditional approach with the Timeline view for more sophisticated arrangements and automation options. There’s even a Key Lock function that lets you play and edit music without hitting a bad note!

![Browse. Discover. Create.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature4_01.jpg)

## Browse. Discover. Create.

Grab the sounds you’re looking for with the intuitive browser. Loops, kits, instruments, and presets are clearly labeled with pack artwork to find the perfect sound. Use the Favorite feature to tag and filter your go-to sounds with a single tap, and explore new tones with weekly browser offers.

![Fresh sounds. Fresh ideas.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature4_02.jpg)

## Fresh sounds. Fresh ideas.

The Zenbeats Store is loaded with sounds, loops, and creative tools to expand your musical palette. Download impactful hip-hop titles like Drill Trap 1 and Sub 808, or explore Sounds of Berlin, Blip Chip, and other electronic collections. Whatever sound or genre you’re chasing, the Zenbeats Store has you covered. All sounds are royalty-free and new tones are added weekly.

## Expand your creativity.

Whatever your experience level or creative role, Zenbeats helps you create music that’s as boundless as your imagination.

![Mix and match.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature3_01.jpg)

### Mix and match.

With the full-screen mixer view, it’s simple to control volume, filter, panning, and more. Browse through 22 native FX, EQ tracks to your liking, and balance all your audio, instrument, and drum tracks from one streamlined location.

![Connect hardware.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature3_02.jpg)

### Connect hardware.

Use Roland’s VERSELAB hardware with Zenbeats for expanded song production. Edit tones with full touch capabilities from the Zenbeats mobile app, organize clips and sections graphically, and freely manipulate sounds. Or use the WM-1 Wireless MIDI Adaptor and connect with hardware like the JUPITER-X, JUPITER-Xm, FANTOM, MC-101, and more via Bluetooth® MIDI.

![Easy sharing.](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_feature3_03.jpg)

### Easy sharing.

Effortless transfers between phone, tablet, and desktop make the distance between you and your finished product even shorter. Use Google Drive™ or OneDrive to share Zenbeats projects and export stems and loops for use in other DAWs.

## Free, unlock, or membership. The choice is yours.

With the free version of Zenbeats, you get music production essentials plus the ability to purchase additional loops and presets in the Zenbeats Store. When you’re ready to expand, there are three ways to unlock the full Zenbeats experience:

*   **Platform Unlock**: Get all features, instruments, and effects on your favorite platform. Platform Unlock includes over 2,500 presets, loops, and sounds (2.5 GB), ZR1 Drum Sampler with sampling/editing, ZC1 Synthesizer with 90 built-in MFX, full SampleVerse modular synthesizer with editor, VST/AU/AUv3 support, and unlimited mixing and export capabilities.
*   **Max Unlock**: Get all features, instruments, effects, and store packs on all platforms and devices. Max Unlock includes over 14,000 presets, loops, and sounds (over 8 GB), ZR1 Drum Sampler with sampling/editing, ZC1 Synthesizer with 90 built-in MFX, SampleVerse modular synthesizer with editor, VST/AU/AUv3 support, and unlimited mixing and export capabilities.
*   **Roland Cloud Membership**: All [Roland Cloud](/cloud/memberships) membership tiers include Zenbeats Max Unlock. Roland Cloud is a Mac and Windows service featuring an ever-evolving collection of legendary Roland software synthesizers, sounds, samples, loops, and access to the wider world of ZEN-Core. With this flexible workflow, you can use the same tones in ZC1, ZENOLOGY, and compatible ZEN-Core hardware.

![](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/img_unlock.jpg)

Download Roland Cloud Manager

[Roland Cloud Manager](https://www.roland.com/download_roland_cloud_manager/) for macOS and Windows gives you access to our entire collection of apps and instruments.

Create a FREE Roland Account  

Choose Any Membership Tier  

Zenbeats Max Unlock will be automatically available.

![Zenbeats App](https://static.roland.com/products/zenbeats/v3/images/ico_app.png)

## Try it for free.  
Choose your platform to download.

# Research: “Beat Fiend” naming landscape

> **Preliminary naming research only. This is not legal advice or trademark clearance.** It is an initial internet and marketplace screen as of July 2026. It does not establish whether any name is registrable or safe to use.

## Summary

“Beat Fiend” is memorable and communicates intense enthusiasm for beats, but it already has an exact, longstanding use in recorded electronic music and several lesser music-related uses. The phrase is also semantically double-edged: “fiend” can mean an enthusiast, but its primary meanings include devil, wicked person, and addict. No obvious exact-name rhythm app surfaced in the searches performed, but the music and rhythm naming field is crowded, and a proper clearance search is still required.

## Findings

1. **High: exact-name collision in recorded music**. Beatport lists Dan Curtin’s *Beatfiend* release on Mobilee Records, released February 16, 2009, with an exact “Beat Fiend” title track. This is a direct search and discovery collision in the same broad music market, even though a song or release title is not automatically a trademark right for software. The release is also currently represented on Bandcamp and Qobuz. [Beatport release](https://www.beatport.com/release/beatfiend/153772) [Bandcamp](https://dancurtin.bandcamp.com/album/beatfiend-2) [Qobuz](https://www.qobuz.com/be-fr/album/beatfiend-dan-curtin/qkssg4pahwfca)

2. **Medium: additional exact and near-exact music uses increase search noise**. Amazon Music carries “Beat Fiend (feat. Trypz)” by Dirty Prescott Kids; SoundCloud has a longstanding `BeatFiend` user identity; and web results preserve a “Beat Fiends Beat Battle” event name. These may be small or inactive uses, but collectively they make the phrase less ownable in search and social discovery. [Amazon Music](https://music.amazon.co.uk/tracks/B08G57FVP3) [SoundCloud](https://soundcloud.com/beatfiend1987/chase-status-loves-theme) [Beat Fiends Beat Battle archive](https://directorzone.cyberlink.com/video/511069)

3. **Medium: no obvious exact-name app was found, but absence is not evidence of availability**. Exact web searches for `"Beat Fiend" app`, `"BeatFiend" rhythm game`, and close variants primarily returned the Dan Curtin release and miscellaneous music uses, not a clearly active app with that exact title. Search engines do not comprehensively index app stores, business registries, unregistered common-law uses, pending marks, or all jurisdictions, so this only lowers the obvious-product-collision concern. The USPTO explicitly says a comprehensive search should cover federal and state records, domain registries, international databases, and internet/common-law use. [USPTO clearance guidance](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/comprehensive-clearance-search-similar-trademarks)

4. **Medium: “fiend” has both useful energy and material negative connotations**. Merriam-Webster defines it first as a devil, demon, or wicked/malicious person, but also as a fanatic, addict, or highly skilled person. “Beat Fiend” therefore reads naturally as “beat obsessive,” which supports an edgy enthusiast brand, but can also evoke addiction, drugs, evil, or aggression. This may be acceptable for a stylized game brand, but is less suitable if the product should feel welcoming, educational, family-friendly, or wellness-oriented. [Merriam-Webster](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/fiend) [Cambridge Dictionary](https://dictionary.cambridge.org/dictionary/english/fiend)

5. **Medium: the phrase is somewhat descriptive in music culture**. “Beat fiend” is used as ordinary language for a devoted producer or listener, not only as a proper name. That helps immediate comprehension but weakens distinctiveness and creates noisy organic search results. Examples include an article titled “The Necessary Evolution of a Beat Fiend” and Vice’s descriptive reference to “production aficionados and beat fiends.” This is a branding observation, not a legal conclusion about descriptiveness. [Printmatic](https://printmatic.net/the-necessary-evolution-of-a-beat-fiend/) [Vice](https://www.vice.com/en/article/blue-daisy-interview-2014-the-mask-and-the-aura-new-album/)

6. **High: “Beat + noun” is the most visibly crowded naming territory**. Major and smaller products include Beat Saber, Beatstar, BeatStars, Beatport, Zenbeats, BEAT MP3, Beat Maker Star, Beat Swiper, and multiple independent games called BeatForge. Another product named BeatForge is especially close in concept: it is described as a browser rhythm game that turns uploaded songs into playable charts. Avoid relying on a generic second word to distinguish another `Beat___` name. [Beat Saber](https://beatsaber.com/) [Beatstar](https://apps.apple.com/au/app/beatstar/id1521483641) [BeatStars](https://www.beatstars.com/) [Roland Zenbeats](https://www.roland.com/us/products/rc_zenbeats/) [BEAT MP3](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/beat-mp3-rhythm-game/id783617527) [BeatForge example](https://kuangshi-tw.itch.io/beatforge)

7. **High: “Rhythm + role/object” and generic music words are crowded too**. Rhythm Doctor is an established game; `Pulse` is used by multiple unrelated music apps; `Groove`, `Loop`, `Tempo`, `Cadence`, `Sync`, and `Melody` produce many music and game results. These territories communicate category quickly but are difficult for search differentiation unless paired with a highly distinctive coined element. [Rhythm Doctor](https://rhythmdr.com/) [Pulse music sharing app](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/pulse-share-your-music/id6450016905) [Pulse Music player](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.hardcodecoder.pulse&hl=en_US) [Group the Loop](https://grouptheloop.com/) [Cadence on Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/app/362800/Cadence/)

8. **High: editor and construction metaphors are also congested**. `Forge`, `Chart`, `Grid`, and `Maker` fit the beatmap-DAW concept but already appear heavily in adjacent tools. ChartForge is specifically a rhythm-game chart editor, NoteGrid and Mixgrid are music-making apps, and BeatForge is already used by several rhythm games. A final name should not simply combine one music term with one construction term. [ChartForge](https://chart-forge.app/app/) [NoteGrid](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/notegrid-make-beats-songs/id6452839894) [Mixgrid](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=app.mixgrid.mixgrid&hl=en_US)

9. **Recommendation**. Treat “Beat Fiend” as a working title with **medium-to-high naming risk**, driven primarily by exact recorded-music collisions, weak search uniqueness, and mixed connotations, not by a confirmed conflicting software trademark. If the edgy identity is central, it may still be worth formal screening. Otherwise, prioritize a coined or unexpected compound that reflects the product’s distinctive loop of charting, jamming, and projectile-style playtesting.

## Ten alternatives for later availability checks

These are ideation candidates, not cleared names. A quick exact-phrase web screen found no obvious exact music-app or rhythm-game match for most of them, but that is a very low bar. Several contain crowded component words and all require app-store, domain, social-handle, business-name, and trademark searches.

1. **Pulsewright**: suggests deliberate rhythm craftsmanship; strongest coined construction in this set.
2. **Jamjectory**: combines live jamming with projectile trajectories; distinctive and product-specific, though pronunciation should be tested.
3. **Chart Cannon**: directly connects beatmap authoring and projectile playtesting; no obvious exact app result surfaced.
4. **Song Siege**: energetic combat framing with room to grow beyond drums.
5. **Tempo Arsenal**: communicates musical timing plus a multi-lane weapon set; both component words need formal screening.
6. **Lane Loom**: suggests weaving notes across lanes; a music sequencer named Loom exists, so this is lower priority despite no exact match. [Loom](http://seanlanefuller.sdf.org/loom/index.html)
7. **Note Barrage**: captures simultaneous projectile notes; “Bassline Barrage” exists as an indie music game, so similarity should be reviewed. [Bassline Barrage](https://github.com/SirBloberto/Bassline-Barrage)
8. **Impact Grid**: emphasizes hit timing and editor structure; generic components make it less distinctive than the coined options.
9. **Downbeat Forge**: clearly evokes beatmap construction and downbeat alignment, but the `Forge` territory is crowded.
10. **Beatmap Bastion**: strongly category-relevant and game-like, but `beatmap` is generic community vocabulary and “Bastion” is already prominent in games, so this is an exploratory candidate only.

**Suggested shortlist order for deeper checking:** Pulsewright, Jamjectory, Chart Cannon, Song Siege, then Tempo Arsenal. Before spending on visual identity, test pronunciation, spelling recall, international connotations, `.com` and relevant TLDs, package names, major social handles, Steam, itch.io, Apple App Store, Google Play, music services, and trademark databases in intended launch markets.

## Sources

### Kept

- [Beatport: Beatfiend by Dan Curtin](https://www.beatport.com/release/beatfiend/153772), direct marketplace evidence of the 2009 exact-title music release.
- [Dan Curtin Bandcamp: Beatfiend](https://dancurtin.bandcamp.com/album/beatfiend-2), artist-controlled presence corroborating continuing discoverability.
- [Merriam-Webster: fiend](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/fiend), authoritative connotation evidence.
- [USPTO: Comprehensive clearance search](https://www.uspto.gov/trademarks/search/comprehensive-clearance-search-similar-trademarks), primary guidance on why this internet screen is not clearance.
- [Apple App Store: BEAT MP3](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/beat-mp3-rhythm-game/id783617527), direct evidence of crowded `Beat` rhythm-app naming and an adjacent bring-your-own-music concept.
- [ChartForge](https://chart-forge.app/app/), direct adjacent-product collision in rhythm chart editing.
- [Roland Zenbeats](https://www.roland.com/us/products/rc_zenbeats/), primary product evidence for crowded `beats` naming in creation software.

### Dropped

- SEO download mirrors for the Dan Curtin release, excluded as redundant and lower-authority than Beatport, Bandcamp, and Qobuz.
- App-keyword analytics pages, excluded because their methods and currentness were unclear.
- Generic “games like” aggregation pages, excluded where direct store or developer pages were available.
- Individual social profiles with no evidence of active commercial use, retained only where useful to illustrate search noise and not treated as legal conflicts.

## Gaps and residual risks

- No search of USPTO, EUIPO, UKIPO, CIPO, WIPO, state registries, or other national trademark records was completed for “Beat Fiend” or the alternatives.
- No attorney assessed likelihood of confusion, classes, related goods and services, common-law priority, or registrability.
- No comprehensive Apple App Store, Google Play, Steam, console storefront, company-register, domain, package-registry, or social-handle audit was completed.
- Search results are localized and incomplete. Exact-name absence can change at any time and does not imply availability.
- Music releases and descriptive phrase uses may or may not create enforceable rights, but they still create discoverability and brand-positioning costs.
- Candidate alternatives received only lightweight exact-web screening. Similar spellings, phonetic equivalents, translations, and related-market marks remain unchecked.

## Acceptance evidence

- **review-findings:** High-severity same-field naming collision documented at Beatport; medium-severity additional music uses and connotation concerns documented; high-severity crowded naming territories documented with direct product URLs.
- **residual-risks:** Formal trademark databases, common-law priority, domains, handles, stores, jurisdictions, and legal analysis remain outstanding and are listed above.
